title: /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/.claude 内容を表示せよ
summary_text: **Compatible Now** - `Macro harness` maps cleanly to [FUGUE.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/FUGUE.md): requirement shaping, prioritization, acceptance judgment, reroute rules. - `Micro harness` maps cleanly to [KERNEL.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/KERNEL.md): decomposition, parallel lanes, implement/test/review execution. - Cross-cutting behavior belongs in [OPERATIONS.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/OPERATIONS.md): response/search/deletion/autonomy rules. - Reusable correction/state retention belongs in [MEMORY.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/MEMORY.md). - Team composition and selective workflow loading belongs in [SKILLS.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/SKILLS.md). **Needs New Doc** - The article’s core missing piece is `Project OS` as a first-class state layer. Add [PROJECT-OS.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/docs/PROJECT-OS.md). - Put in that doc: canonical state graph (`KPI -> Story -> Spec -> Ticket -> Subtask -> Artifact/PR`), acceptance fields, status transitions, and state ownership. - Add a compact schema companion (for templates/contracts), e.g. [PROJECT-OS-SCHEMA.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/docs/PROJECT-OS-SCHEMA.md). - Add short pointers from [FUGUE.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/FUGUE.md) and [KERNEL.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/KERNEL.md): when to read/write Project OS. - Encode “single-lane fallback is invalid for non-trivial execution” in Project OS workflow checks to avoid regression. **Should Not Go Into Root Policy Files** - Long conceptual narrative, historical trial-and-error, or opinionated essays. - Detailed ticket schemas, YAML examples, runbooks, and tool-specific wiring. - Provider/tool advocacy text (“use X platform”) and marketing-style framing. - Implementation-level operating procedures that change frequently. If you want, I can implement `PROJECT-OS.md` + schema + pointer updates in the current split next.
